behaviors.description: History is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages before LLM invocation when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily deterministic boundary logic (message windowing/order). A focused unit test can catch off-by-one, wrong ordering, or wrong slice direction without real I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Test the history-preparation function/object directly with 11-20 prior messages and assert exactly the latest 10 (in correct chronological order) are passed to the LLM request payload builder.

behaviors.description: Generated response is relevant to the customer’s question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM semantic behavior, which is non-deterministic and quality-based. Mocks cannot reveal generic/off-topic drift.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated set of real customer queries against the production-configured LLM prompt, score relevance with a rubric/judge, and fail if relevance falls below threshold or off-topic rate exceeds threshold across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal response for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The detection claim is model-judgment quality, not only wiring. A mock classifier could always pass and hide real misclassification.
behaviors.test_description: Provide known overly formal candidate replies for casual-tier context to the real tone-check prompt/model, verify they are flagged, and assert the orchestration issues a regeneration call when flagged.

behaviors.description: Full exchange (user input, first draft, regenerations, final response) is persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is module wiring to an actual DB schema and timestamp handling; in-memory mocks can miss ORM mapping, transaction, and timestamp serialization issues.
behaviors.test_description: Run chatbot flow with regeneration enabled against a real local test DB, then verify persisted records include all exchange events with expected foreign keys/order and valid timestamps (monotonic and correctly stored format/timezone).

behaviors.description: If tone-triggered regeneration occurs, only the regenerated response is returned to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-selection behavior across components (generation, tone gate, response emitter). Mocks at a single-function level can miss cross-module leakage.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end application modules in-process with controlled model outputs (first draft flagged, second accepted) and assert customer-facing output channel/API returns only second response while first exists only in internal logs.
